_doc: RANK-16 resolution sweep — HIGH-RES half, runs on the big boy (studentresearch2, RTX 6000 Ada 48GB, /home/studentresearch2). Same recipe as the low-res half (lora_rank 16 / alpha 32, fp32 base/amp defaults for one continuous resolution curve; per-epoch val on the default 10% IMD split — full IMD is reported only in the eval sweep). Extends the original sweep with three NEW cells above 688 (768/896/1024) that only fit in 48 GB. Effective batch 8 everywhere. The Ada has plenty of room (49 GB/card); micro-batch is raised to 8 wherever it fits to cut accum loops — 375 steps/epoch is unchanged so the LR schedule is byte-identical. NOTE: while the 7B run occupies GPU 0, launch this on GPU 1 only (GPU=1). The top two cells are pinned to batch 4/ga 2 as an OOM cushion under the two-pass val-zoom spike; with GPU 1 fully free they can safely go to 8/1. Launch: GPU=1 RUNS_ROOT=/home/studentresearch2/runs/ablation bash run_scripts/run_ablation_resolution.sh (point --queue at this file).
